cmes.note: Narrow CME visible to the west in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The source is an eruption seen around S10W130 in STEREO A EUV 304 imagery starting at 2026-06-20T00:45Z, directly following a data gap from 2026-06-19T19:15Z to 2026-06-20T00:45Z. The eruption is also seen beyond the west limb in SDO AIA 304 and GOES SUVI 284/304 starting around 2026-06-20T00:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on source location.

cmes.note: CME visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an M1.3 flare from AR 14472 (S12E70) peaking at 2026-06-20T01:51Z. An eruption with dimming and opening field lines is best seen in SDO AIA 193 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source location.

cmes.note: CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-2 and the north in STEREO A COR2. The source is an eruption beyond the NE limb as seen in GOES SUVI 304 imagery starting at 2026-06-20T02:49Z. Moving field lines are also visible in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source seen in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Faint CME seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2. The CME is not clearly visible in other coronagraph imagery, and the leading edge is faint. The potential source may be a region of dimming seen around N00W45 starting around 2026-06-20T11:00Z in SDO AIA 193 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2 running difference imagery.

cmes.note: Very faint, narrow CME first seen to the NNE part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-20T20:00Z. It is also seen to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C3 and briefly in GOES CCOR-1. It is not seen in STEREO A COR2. No source candidate was found for this CME on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Very faint, wide CME visible to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 only. The CME disappears half-way through the C2 field of view. The potential source for this CME is the M2.6 flare peaking at 2026-06-21T02:46Z from Active Region 14473 (S08E67) as seen in SDO/AIA 131. There is an associated failed filament eruption in SDO/AIA 304 starting around 2026-06-21T2:39Z through 2026-06-21T03:30Z. Additionally, there are moving/opening field lines visible in SDO/AIA 131 starting around 2026-06-21T02:55Z which appear to be successful in releasing from the lower corona.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on source location and best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. The CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to this, some uncertainty with parameters is present.

cmes.note: Narrow CME visible to the W in STEREO A COR2 imagery. It may be faintly visible to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 for a frame, but it is hard to tell for sure. The source of this CME appears to be filament ejecta from along the Western limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-06-21T07:15Z, possibly associated with old Active Regions 14464 and 14467 beyond the SW limb from STEREO A's point of view.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using STEREO A COR2 imagery and using SOHO LASCO C2's view of the possible associated front. Higher degree of uncertainty due to limited viewpoints.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the W/SW in STEREO A COR2 and to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source of this CME may be from activity beyond the W/SW limb as seen in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery, possibly associated with old Active Regions 14464 and 14467 beyond the SW limb from STEREO A's point of view.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Narrow CME visible to the W in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be associated with eruption activity from beyond the W/SW limb, possibly associated with old ARs 14464/14467 starting around 2026-06-21T10:15Z in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on potential source location with variability in longitude likely (+/- 10 to 15 degrees) due to limited viewpoints.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the SW in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be associated with eruption activity from beyond the W/SW limb, possibly associated with old Active Regions 14464 and 14467.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the west in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a far-sided eruption visible near the western limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-06-21T16:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the approximate source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the west in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a far-sided eruption visible near the western limb in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-06-21T18:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source longitude.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east/southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-06-21T19:53Z. The source is an M6.8 flare from Active Region 14473 (S09E57) starting around 2026-06-21T19:25Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. An EUV wave is observed surrounding the eruption site in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195 at this time as well.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source longitude. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the Northeast by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-21T23:00Z. This CME is not clearly seen in other coronagraphs. The source is a far-sided eruption visible over the northeast limb starting around 2026-06-21T21:33Z seen in SDO AIA 171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 due to source from beyond the NE limb and lack of coronagraph imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: Narrow and faint CME visible to the West in STEREO A COR2 imagery and faintly visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 difference imagery. The source of this CME is not clearly seen due to a data gap prior to the CME start time, but may possibly be associated with eruption activity from the area of old Active Regions 14464 and 14467 beyond the W/SW limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Not clearly visible in GOES CCOR-1. This CME is possibly associated with a small filament eruption starting around 2026-06-22T00:00Z from an unnumbered region near N07E32. This location is in close proximity to an equatorial coronal hole to the West of the source region, and the CME may be deflected to the east as a result.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the potential source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the SW in STEREO A COR2 imagery and faintly to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source of this CME appears to be filament ejecta from farsided eruption activity possibly associated with old Active Regions 14464 and 14467.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the SW in STEREO A COR2 imagery and faintly to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source of this CME appears to be filament ejecta from farsided eruption activity possibly associated with old Active Regions 14464 and 14467. This CME overlaps the CME first seen to the SW in STEREO A COR2 imagery at 2026-06-22T03:23Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 due to being far sided from the STEREO A perspective. The source of this CME is a filament eruption visible to the E in GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-06-22T06:17Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 due to source near the limb and lack of visible STEREO A imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the WSW only in STEREO A COR2, likely due to the faint front and being blocked by the SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 occulting disks. The source is unclear but likely far-sided, only potentially seen as a brightening prominence on the west limb in STEREO A COR2 EUVI 304 at 2026-06-22T07:15Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 plane-of-sky taken atm W151.9 with STEREO A at W61.942. This analysis encompasses the wider of two fronts that appear at this same time, blending together after the first two frames and becoming indistinguishable.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery for a few frames before the leading edge becomes too diffuse to observe. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures visible on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source for this event is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth or STEREO A-facing disk around the time of this event. A very faint opening of field lines may be visible beyond the southwest limb in STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery starting around 2026-06-23T08:05Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 152 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures visible on the Earth or STEREO A-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 152 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: Loop CME seen to the east-northeast in SOHO LASCO C2, C3 and STEREO A COR2A. The CME appears to be associated with eruptive activity over the eastern limb, as evidenced by opening field lines bending from eastward to the northeast seen in SDO AIA 171 imagery starting around 2026-06-23T18:00Z. The full extent of the eruption is better visualized in GOES SUVI 284 imagery, where a faint loop seen stretching from northeast to southeast begins erupting outward off the eastern limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Fairly speculative leading edge measurement using only Sun-Earth line coronagraphs which include SOHO and GOES with no STEREO Ahead imagery, therefore, there is high uncertainty in source longitude by up to 15 degrees.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-06-23T01:26Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This CME is mostly blocked by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is a region of dimming seen centered at approx. N40E50 beginning at approx. 2026-06-24T00:49Z as seen in SDO AIA 193 with some likely deflection towards the Northwest.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the NE by GOES CCOR-1 beginning at 2026-06-24T13:00Z. This CME is not clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a downlink data gap from 2026-06-24T06:24Z to 2026-06-24T15:36Z, and is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to it being far sided from the COR2A perspective. The source of this event is a small eruption from Active Region 14475 (S09E32) beginning at approx. 2026-06-24T10:00Z as seen in SDO AIA 131 and 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint, thin CME observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, more faintly in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely triggered by AR 14477 (S13E55), with two weak brightenings observed at S11E55 and N00E40 and an area of dimming in between centered around S01E51, all in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-06-25T23:16Z, also observed in SDO AIA 171 and GOES SUVI 171 as field line movement originating from this area.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Half width is less than 10 degrees in SOHO, appearing slightly more wide in STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and potentially to the SW in STEREO A COR2, it is difficult to resolve but a faint feature is visible in COR2A white light imagery only. No direct source is observed for this event, likely far-sided, and may be visible as weak field line movement in SDO AIA 171 around 2026-06-25T03:31Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane-of-sky due to the faint front. While a faint signature is observed in STEREO A COR2 white light, it is not resolvable in running difference imagery to reliably triangulate with.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The front starts slow and faint in LASCO C2, becoming more defined further into the respective fields of view. No distinct source is observed for this event, an eruption in SDO AIA 193 from AR 14470 at 2026-06-25T14:37Z was a culprit but occurs too late for the slow start time of this front.

cmeAnalyses.note: GOES CCOR-1 only plane of sky as the CME is blocked by the LASCO C3 pylon.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2, not yet visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a downlink gap and GOES CCOR-1 due to a data gap. The source is a filament eruption over the SW limb from Earth, and on-disk for STEREO A. Observed as filament ejecta over the SW limb in SDO AIA 304 starting at 2026-06-25T13:25Z and also in GOES SUVI 304. Observed on-disk brightening and field line movement in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 starting at 2026-06-25T13:25Z near former AR 14469, putting the approximate center filament location ~S15W115. Also observed as field line movement and filament ejecta in SDO AIA 131/171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between STEREO A COR2, and available SOHO LASCO C3 imagery at this time due to a downlink gap.
Preliminary approximation of the far sided source around W115 as viewed in STEREO A EUVI, could vary W105-W125. STEREO A COR2 only observing coronagraph at this time.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-25T16:36Z. It is also seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and to the SW in STEREO A COR2. No independent source has been found for this CME. It is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-06-25T14:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Narrow front first seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-25T19:36Z. It is also seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is not seen in STEREO A COR2. This front is most likely additional outflow following CME: 2026-06-25T14:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to lack of triangulation.

cmes.note: Very narrow CME first seen to the E in GOES CCOR-1 starting around 2026-06-25T21:15Z. It is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3. It is covered by real time data gaps in STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2. The source of this CME is most likely some nearly continuous eruptive activity from AR 14478 (S06E64).

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on probable, on-disk source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the W part way into the FOV of STEREO A COR2. This CME is only seen in STEREO A COR2, and the start is covered by a real-time data gap. The start of this CME is also covered by a data gap in STEREO A EUVI. The front is only visible for three frames and is later overtaken by CME: 2026-06-25T23:48Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very speculative plane-of-sky measurement based on three frames from STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the NW in GOES CCOR-1 starting around 2026-06-25T21:24Z. It is mostly obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3 and is covered by real-time data gaps in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely some minor field line movement seen beyond the NW limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning around 2026-06-25T21:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to far-sided source candidate and lack of triangulation.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-25T23:00Z. It is also seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is not seen in STEREO A COR2. No source candidate has been found on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement due to lack of triangulation.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-25T23:48Z. This leading edge is extremely faint in SOHO LASCO C2 and is not seen in SOHO LASCO C3 or GOES CCOR-1. The front is best seen to the W in STEREO A COR2. Measurement indicates that the source of this CME is far-sided, but the start time is covered by data gaps in STEREO A EUVI.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-26T00:24Z. It is also seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is not seen in STEREO A COR2. The source is most likely some opening field lines seen beyond the limb in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-06-25T23:34Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: 3D SOHO LASCO C2/C3 analysis using approximate source of far-sided filament, with updated Solar Orbiter EUVI 304 imagery observing a likely filament eruption spanning approximately E130-E90 (centered around E110, used here). Longitude could vary slightly as a result.
Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of triangulation and beyond-limb source.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source is a farsided filament eruption centered around approximately S15W120, near former AR 14469. Observed as filament ejecta and moving field lines over the SW limb in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 starting around 2026-06-26T01:16Z. Also observed on-disk as filament ejecta, brightening, and field line movement from former AR 14469 in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 starting around 2026-06-26T01:15Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and SW in STEREO A COR2. No distinct source for this CME is observed, but triangulates well to the far-side around E144 via the swpc_cat tool.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2, and faintly in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. No source is observed for this event, but it does closely follow and present similarly to prior CME 2026-06-26T00:24Z at E110 and CME 2026-06-26T02:24Z at E144.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 plane-of-sky due to the unseen far-sided source and no STEREO A COR2 visbility to triangulate.

cmes.note: Streamer blowout CME observed to the W in STEREO A COR2, and as a faint halo in SOHO LASCO C2 better observed on the northern halo edge. No distinct source for this CME is observed, and is likely far-sided. The bright west streamer in STEREO A COR2 blows out following the leading edge of this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 and the faint halo signature in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the NE in STEREO A COR2 starting around 2026-06-26T05:08Z. It is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2. There is not a definitive source candidate for this CME, but it is possibly related to some very minor field line fluctuation near the eastern footpoint of a filament centered around N27E10 beginning a couple hours prior to the CME start time best seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative triangulated measurement based on limited imagery from STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-26T08:24Z. It is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. No definitive source candidate has been identified. It is possible that this feature could be related to some activity starting around 2026-06-26T06:10Z near AR 14478, but the timing makes this unlikely.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of source candidate and lack of triangulation.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The front starts very faint in LASCO C2 and develops more clearly further into the respective coronagraphs' field-of-view. The source is faint but likely field line movement and brightening from former AR 14468 observed in STEREO A EUVI 195 at 2026-06-26T05:35Z, and subtly over the NW limb in SDO AIA 193 around ~2026-06-26T05:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and SW in STEREO A COR2. No source for this CME is observed, likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The front is quickly overlapped in STEREO A COR2 by CME 2026-06-26T15:09Z. The source is likely dimming and field line movement centered around S30W90, observed best in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting around 2026-06-26T13:35Z, with likely westward deflection caused by the nearby coronal hole east of the eruption.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2, and as a partial halo to the S/W in SOHO LASCO C2. The source is far-sided from Earth, likely a newly developed active region observed ~W160 in Solar Orbiter PHI at 2026-06-26T05:39Z. This source is observed in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 starting around 2026-06-26T13:35Z as field line movement and filament ejecta on or just beyond the SW limb, which is ~W152.4 with STEREO A at W62.4. Likely associated with a GOES-equivalent M1.0 [C6,M2] flare observed in Solar Orbiter STIX peaking at 2026-06-26T14:35Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Shock triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Bulk triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-06-26T19:12Z. It is also seen to the SE in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. It is mostly missed in real time by SOHO LASCO C3 due to a gap between downlinks. There are a couple source candidates for this CME. The first is some minor field line movement near the SE limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning as early as 2026-06-26T17:48Z. The second candidate is near-continuous activity and fluctuation from AR 14478 that is best seen in SDO AIA 193 for hours prior to and following the CME start time.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulated measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the W in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-06-26T19:34Z. It is only seen in STEREO A COR2A. The majority of the propagation is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely an eruption seen near the W limb in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting as early as 2026-06-26T18:35Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to beyond-limb source candidate and lack of triangulation.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the S in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-06-26T21:12Z. It is also seen to the S in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and to the SE in STEREO A COR2. The most likely source candidate for this CME is a region of southward directed dimming best seen in SDO AIA 195 starting around 2026-06-26T17:34Z centered around S30E05. There is also some field line movement likely associated with this dimming over the S limb best seen in SDO AIA 171 beginning around 2026-06-26T20:06Z and in GOES SUVI 284 beginning around 2026-06-26T19:49Z. A much less likely source candidate for this CME is associated with a C2.4 class flare from AR 14475 (S09W04) that peaked at 2026-06-26T20:21Z. The flare is best seen in SDO AIA 131, but the eruption followed by some post eruptive arcades is best seen in SDO AIA 304 and SDO AIA 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: PRELIMINARY measurement based on triangulated best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 as well as on most probable source location.
